title: Should I watch the rebuilds
wikitext:
[]: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by SpaceCowboy35 » Wed Nov 07, 2018 8:12 am I am curious about its quality

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Nov 07, 2018 8:37 am By all means watch the Rebuild films if you're prepared to take them on their own terms. But if you will only consider them as a remake of the series you are likely to get very annoyed when you come to the aspects of them which are not.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by Joseki » Wed Nov 07, 2018 1:33 pm If you can wait 3 years unspoiled you should wait for the fourth movie to be available with english subtitles. If you can't you should watch them, they get a lot of underserved hate just because they are named Evangelion and everyone expect them to be NGE with better graphics.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Wed Nov 07, 2018 1:48 pm SpaceCowboy35 wrote: quality

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by ShonHam » Thu Nov 08, 2018 6:26 am It definitely adds to the overall experience in my personal opinion and allows you to join in more forms of conversation. If you think of and measure them based on their own merits and without allowing the original to sour your experience they're definitely worth while and of quality.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Nov 08, 2018 10:36 am Watch the first two. Then wait about a year. Do something else, I dunno. Watch some other trash anime or something. Then watch the first two again. Wait about another year. Have some fun. Read a book or whatever. Then, what the heck, watch the first two a third time. They’re fun and great, right? Right after that watch the third one. It’s weird, confusing, and extremely jarring. The film presents a rollercoaster of a ride that is hard to recover from. Sometime after watching the third film, rewatch the first two yet again. You’ll notice that the first two movies aren’t quite the same as they were before you watched the third one. Misato’s a different character entirely, and Rei is an even stronger anomaly than she ever was in the TV show. It’s almost like watching the third film for the first time summoned Anno himself to sneak into your movie collection while you were unaware and replace the first two films with other versions that are exactly the same, only different. You can never see these movies the same way ever again, and you miss the times when you could see them as they once were. I'm looking forward to something similar happening with Shin Eva's release. If I set enough traps, I can perhaps catch the summoned Anno as he's sneaking around my house replacing my Eva discs.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by SpaceCowboy35 » Thu Nov 08, 2018 11:06 am ErgoProxy wrote: 1.0: Rei shows ass, 2.0: Misato shows ass, 3.0: Lilith shows ass, 4.0: ...?!

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 08, 2018 12:14 pm With 3.0, both in terms of its direction, and conclusion, I would recommend waiting for 3.0+1.0 to be released so you can watch them all without significant interruption. Do you want to watch three films in a week and then wait ~3 years to watch the fourth one?

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by SpaceCowboy35 » Thu Nov 08, 2018 12:18 pm 1. huge fan for about a year now 2. Couldn't I just rewatch them when it comes out? 3. If I do, how do I avoid spoilers

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Nov 11, 2018 10:51 pm ErgoProxy wrote: 1.0: Rei shows ass, 2.0: Misato shows ass, 3.0: Lilith shows ass, 4.0: ...?!

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Mon Nov 12, 2018 2:27 pm I think that someone has already found the answer …

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Nov 12, 2018 8:12 pm ^ oh, daddy.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Mon Nov 12, 2018 10:00 pm ErgoProxy wrote: I think that someone has already found the answer …

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Nov 16, 2018 3:51 pm ErgoProxy wrote: 1.0: Rei shows ass, 2.0: Misato shows ass, 3.0: Lilith shows ass, 4.0: ...?! But if you're asking about technical quality of animation and stuff - sure, Rebuilds are way better than the original series.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by TBsq » Mon Nov 19, 2018 6:41 pm It's exactly as I thought it would be. Not good.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Nov 20, 2018 2:59 am Care to expand on that?

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by TBsq » Tue Nov 20, 2018 6:35 am All the things that I like about Evangelion are gone in these films, like the direction, the tension that builds up, the cinematography, the sometimes slow pacing, the experimental side, the dive into the psyche of the characters. And I don't like modern digital animation.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Nov 20, 2018 10:13 am TBsq wrote: the tension that builds up, the cinematography

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by TBsq » Tue Nov 20, 2018 10:54 am I don't like it, there's too much empasis on the movement rather that the subtlety. for example the cinematography in episode 4 is one of the best of the series and there's nothing like that in the rebuilds. Even the action sequences in EoE are way more subtle, for me the scene with Bach Air II feels like a choregraphy more like vulgar "crash boom bang". But maybe I feel like that because I really don't like its modern digital look.

[]: Re: Should I watch the rebuilds?             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Nov 20, 2018 11:48 am I feel like this thread would be the better place to discuss these details
